title: Harris to announce VP pick and Google loses antitrust case: Morning Rundown
summary:
Kamala Harris is expected to announce her running mate today, ahead of their first rally together. The vice president is meeting with candidates, aiming to choose someone who can appeal to white men concerned about voting for Harris while also attracting Biden's base.

In a major antitrust ruling, a federal judge has found that Google holds an illegal monopoly in search engines and text advertisements. The tech giant faces accusations of coercing users to use its search engine and prioritizing its own ads, hindering competition. Google plans to appeal the decision.

The final week of the Paris Olympics has begun with dramatic events in women's gymnastics. Simone Biles won silver in the floor exercise while Jordan Chiles earned bronze after appealing her initial score. Brazil's Rebeca Andrade claimed the gold, and Biles and Chiles displayed sportsmanship by bowing to Andrade during the medal ceremony.
